content:
Question Content There has been a debate on whether we should study in groups or study alone to be more effective. While self-learning do brings lots of benefits, I believe that group study is a better method to acquire new knowledges.
On the one hand, self-learning can be very effective when you are deep-diving into a specific area of knowledge, where only a few people have heard about. It would be quite insufficient to discuss some uncommon topics with someone who can’t really relate themselves to. Besides, self-learning is essential when someone needs to concentrate. When someone is preparing for an exam, it is better not to find some revise partner since every exam point is in the textbook.
But one person’s knowledge base is limited because it is determined by the physical limitation of our brain, and that’s where group study comes in. Group study is an efficient means to complement our knowledge and ideas together, forming a bigger database that can benefit every member of the group. Through the process of communicating with each other, we can gradually form lots of beneficial traits. For instance, we can foster critical thinking while debating with different opinions, along with effective communication skills. Like Confucious once said: “Among three people walking together, there will always be one who can be my teacher”, which is a perfect summary of the benefits of group studying.
To summarize, I believe that studying alone can be useful and effective at some times, but eventually we need to study together to gain more knowledges and make progress together.
summary:
Question

Content

There has been a debate on whether we should study in groups or study alone to be more effective. While self-learning do brings lots of benefits, I believe that group study is a better method to acquire new knowledges.

On the one hand, self-learning can be very effective when you are deep-diving into a specific area of knowledge, where only a few people have heard about. It would be quite insufficient to discuss some uncommon topics with someone who can’t really relate themselves to. Besides, self-learning is essential when someone needs to concentrate. When someone is preparing for an exam, it is better not to find some revise partner since every exam point is in the textbook.
title: Passage 4

content:
IELTS writing Some people argue that the money spent by governments on space programmes would be better spent on vital public services such as schools and hospitals. I partly agree with this statement. I believe that space programmes also brings long-term benefits to society and should not be cut completely.
On the one hand, it is true that those vital public services are the backbone of our society and it should be the top agenda for every government. Schools provide knowledge and skills for children and teenagers, while hospitals have the duty to protect citizen’s health and save lives. If these public services are underfunded, the whole society would suffer. Therefore, when a government’s budget is limited, these vital public services should be the top priority to consider.
On the other hand, this does not mean that those space programmes are waste of money. Space missions can bring strategical long-term benefits. For instance, satellites orbiting the earth are used for weather forecasting, global communication, disaster monitoring, etc. These are essential part of our daily life that bring us convenience and safety. These satellites are useful in our daily life and can also help governments to deal with serious natural issues such as storms, floods and climate change. Additionally, space projects encourage young people to devote themselves into technology, engineering and science, which can benefit a country’s future development.
To summarize, I partly agree with the view that schools and hospitals should get more financial supports from the government, especially when they are lack of basic funds. However, that doesn’t mean space programmes are all useless and should be completely abandoned, since space projects can still bring actual scientific, practical and educational benefits.
summary:
IELTS writing

Some people argue that the money spent by governments on space programmes would be better spent on vital public services such as schools and hospitals. I partly agree with this statement. I believe that space programmes also brings long-term benefits to society and should not be cut completely.

On the one hand, it is true that those vital public services are the backbone of our society and it should be the top agenda for every government. Schools provide knowledge and skills for children and teenagers, while hospitals have the duty to protect citizen’s health and save lives. If these public services are underfunded, the whole society would suffer. Therefore, when a government’s budget is limited, these vital public services should be the top priority to consider.
title: Passage 3

content:
IELTS Writing Task 2 Question The money spent by governments on space programmes would be better spent on vital public services such as schoolss and hospitals.
To what extent do you agree or disagree.
Example Some people believe that the government money spent on space programs should instead be used for essential public services, such as schools and hospitals. I partly agree with this view because education and healthcare should clearly be priorities, but I also believe that space exploration should continue to receive a reasonable level of funding.
On the one hand, public services are more closely connected with people’s daily lives. Schools provide children with knowledge and skills, while hospitals protect people’s health and save lives. If these services are underfunded, the whole society may suffer. For example, poor schools may produce less qualified workers in the future, and overcrowded hospitals may fail to treat patients quickly. Therefore, when a government has limited budget, it should first make sure that basic services are properly funded.
On the other hand, this does not mean that space programmes are a waste of money. Space research can bring important long-term benefits. For instance, satellites are used for weather forecasting, global communication, navigation and disaster monitoring. These technologies are useful in ordinary life and can also help governments deal with serious problems such as storms, floods and and climate change. In addition, space projects can encourage young people to study science and engineering, which may benefit a country’s future development.
In conclusion, I partly agree that schools and hospitals should receive more attention from governments, especially when they lack sufficient funding. However, space programmes should not be completely abandoned, as they can bring scientific, practical and educational benefits. A balanced approach is therefore the best solution.
错误总结 时态，议论文一般不会使用过去式，采用一般现在时。 不要偷换概念，原题限定了public services和space programmes的对立关系，就不要偷换概念或采用其二级概念，如将space programmes偷换成scientific experiment，public services用teachers（二级概念）代指等。 观点的衔接，在brainstorm阶段可以无序地列出自己的想法和观点，但最后要想好先后顺序，注意观点之间的逻辑和衔接关系。 用书面词汇和语法，积累好的词汇和语法。 注意字数不少于250词
summary:
IELTS Writing Task 2

Question

The money spent by governments on space programmes would be better spent on vital public services such as schoolss and hospitals.

To what extent do you agree or disagree.

Example

Some people believe that the government money spent on space programs should instead be used for essential public services, such as schools and hospitals. I partly agree with this view because education and healthcare should clearly be priorities, but I also believe that space exploration should continue to receive a reasonable level of funding.
title: 雅思议论文范文以及错误总结
